description: Cleanup-only release. `writeSharedHooks` and `collectSharedHooks` now read from a single capability table (`SHARED_HOOKS_BY_PLATFORM` in `packages/cli/src/templates/shared-hooks/index.ts`) that declares which shared hook each platform actually registers. Prior releases leaked `statusline.py` into 7 non-Claude platforms (cursor / codex / gemini / qoder / copilot / codebuddy / droid) and leaked `session-start.py` + `inject-workflow-state.py` into Kiro, even though none of those platforms' `hooks.json`/`settings.json`/agent-JSON actually referenced those files. This manifest ships 10 `safe-file-delete` entries, hash-verified against every historical shared-hooks content since the layer was introduced in 0.5.0-beta.0, to remove those orphans on upgrade. Locally-modified hook files are preserved with a warning. No user-visible behavior change on any platform â€” the deleted files were never invoked.
changelog:
**Bug Fixes:**
- fix(cli): `writeSharedHooks` + `collectSharedHooks` stop distributing shared hooks that the target platform's config never references. Both paths now consult a per-platform capability table (`SHARED_HOOKS_BY_PLATFORM`) instead of hand-rolled exclude lists in each configurator. Closes a drift hazard where the runtime-install path and the `trellis update` diff path could diverge silently.
- chore(cli): remove 10 orphaned shared-hook files left behind by the prior distribution logic:
  - `statusline.py` on cursor / codex / gemini / qoder / copilot / codebuddy / droid / kiro (the `statusLine` event is Claude Code only; no other platform invokes this script)
  - `session-start.py` + `inject-workflow-state.py` on kiro (Kiro exposes only `agentSpawn`; no SessionStart or UserPromptSubmit equivalent)
  All removals are `safe-file-delete` entries covering every historical shared-hooks content hash since 0.5.0-beta.0 â€” locally-modified hook files are preserved.

**Internal:**
- `PULL_BASED_HOOK_EXCLUDE` constant deleted; its rule is now encoded in the capability table alongside the Claude-only / Kiro-only rules.
- `writeSharedHooks(hooksDir, platform)` signature simplified: `exclude` option removed, second argument is a `SharedHookPlatform` id.
- `collectSharedHooks(hooksPath, platform)` mirrors the same signature.
- Copilot configurator drops its hand-written `getSharedHookScripts()` loop in favor of the shared helper.

migrations.description: Removed: Cursor has no statusLine event; shared-hooks/statusline.py was never invoked.
Removed: Codex has no statusLine event; shared-hooks/statusline.py was never invoked.
Removed: Gemini CLI has no statusLine event; shared-hooks/statusline.py was never invoked.
Removed: Qoder has no statusLine event; shared-hooks/statusline.py was never invoked.
Removed: GitHub Copilot has no statusLine event; shared-hooks/statusline.py was never invoked.
Removed: CodeBuddy has no statusLine event; shared-hooks/statusline.py was never invoked.
Removed: Factory Droid has no statusLine event; shared-hooks/statusline.py was never invoked.
Removed: Kiro has no statusLine event; shared-hooks/statusline.py was never invoked.
Removed: Kiro supports only `agentSpawn`; there is no SessionStart event to trigger this script.
Removed: Kiro supports only `agentSpawn`; there is no UserPromptSubmit event to trigger this script.

notes: Pure cleanup release. No src/ runtime behavior change on any platform â€” the deleted files were orphans. After `trellis update`, each supported platform's `<platformDir>/hooks/` contains only the shared hooks that platform's own `hooks.json` / `settings.json` / agent-JSON actually references. Users who modified a shared hook locally will see the file preserved with a warning in `trellis update` output.
